key_points: On-campus dorms exist but commuter culture dominates student life
Living on campus means constant exposure to cult recruiters and solicitors
Parking near dorms is a daily frustration with hit-and-run incidents common
key_quotes: I live on campus so I know exactly what you mean. I ignore them but once I step out my dorm it's like you gotta be ready to talk to crazy people in disguise.
Okay maybe I'm not missing out by dorming
one thing about being a csn student is not having to stress about parking. but i am transferring so all hell will be mine

key_points: Overwhelmingly a commuter school — most students arrive, attend class, and leave
Multiple students describe campus as cold, unsocial, and harder than community college
Active student community exists but requires deliberate effort to find clubs and groups
key_quotes: It's crazy how not social this place is... wow. I never realized how quiet the place can be, people just don't want to be social.
I'm just trying to pass.
Join a club or something, sure the majority of people come and go but the core community of UNLV is very social.

key_points: Dining commons food is edible and affordable but quality is inconsistent
Peak hours create serious traffic flow and crowding problems
Breakfast options mocked online; made-to-order stations are the hidden workaround
key_quotes: Just strait up sponge... Spongebob ahh eggs with no seasoning
I have no meal plan whatsoever and get to eat all I can eat for 8 bucks on some days, and all the food is at least decent?
Rookie mistake not getting the made to order eggs at the pancake/egg station

key_points: Students visibly burned out — senior burnout and chronic exhaustion are recurring themes
Grief and personal crises handled unevenly; professor communication is key to survival
Emotional support posts go viral on the subreddit, suggesting unmet need on campus
key_quotes: I'm in my last semester. I only have 2 classes but I've been here for 6 years now. I've been burnt out since year 4.
Life is not easy one bit but you're still fighting the odds. We're in it together!
Always, always communicate with your instructors when something happens. It's difficult but most are understanding.

key_points: Financial aid disbursement posts go viral — students clearly depend on refund timing
Tuition increases happening regularly with no student input or accountability
Nevada in-state students can get full rides through honors/merit scholarships
key_quotes: MY FINANCIAL AID REFUND HIT MY BANK ACCOUNT RN
Ugh… I had to take a break from school to save some money. I may not even return. Education is becoming less affordable every year.
What happened? Only a few years ago an annual pass was like $130. Good thing I graduated.

key_points: Math and CS departments widely criticized — high failure rates are documented and public
Professor quality is wildly inconsistent; Rate My Professor is heavily relied upon
Hospitality program is genuinely world-ranked; most other programs are average at best
key_quotes: The math department is ass. When I look up some of my old teachers and see [the grade distributions]... God damn that's a lot of Fs.
UNLv's College of Education is a joke. Out of 4 classes, I had two clowns.
Fuck Han
This is great gg... I'm a math and CS major and this is incredibly helpful especially since the math department is ass.

key_points: Canvas constantly logs students out — Okta Verify authentication is universally hated
Parking permit costs jumped from ~$130 to $354 annually with no improvement in parking
Board of Regents raised tuition without student input; debris sits unremoved for 4+ years
key_quotes: THEY ACT LIKE WE HAVE NUCLEAR CODES IN OUR SYSTEM I PROMISE THERES NOTHING VALUABLE IN OUR EMAILS.
There's no reason for every single student on campus to pay $354 every year for parking.
This has been on the ground near Lied Library since 2022. It's 2026 and it's still not removed.

key_points: Campus is beautiful with stunning desert sunsets but surrounded by aggressive solicitors
Parking garages are genuinely dangerous — speeding, hit-and-runs, and crashes are routine
Beloved feral cat colony gives campus unexpected charm and community bonding
key_quotes: People do be treating that garage like their private race track.
I've seen like 10 crashes at this exact spot since the start of the semester.
No filters…sometimes nature aches with beauty
atp, strays should be our mascot! definitely rebellious

key_points: Hospitality grads land Strip jobs but the job market post-2025 is tough even for them
CS and engineering grads face a brutal market; networking and internships are essential
Alumni community is present but not strong — UNLV name carries limited prestige outside Nevada
key_quotes: Took me 100 job applications, 7 months post graduation, and moving to a new city to get this job.
I graduated in May with a degree in Hospitality Management (awful timing for that with tourism down 10%+ this year, I know).
It is NOT impossible to get a job as a college grad in 2025... it is HIGHLY improbable.

key_points: Nevada residents with strong stats can get a full ride — exceptional value if you qualify
Out-of-state students get little financial aid; hard to justify cost over better-funded options
Parking fees, tuition hikes, and tech fees add up fast beyond base tuition
key_quotes: UNLV — accepted + full ride + honors college
as a grad student, everything is out of pocket since it's considered a personal investment, so we don't get refunds.
i'm mad asf that we pay this much for parking and they took away 2 whole ass lanes in lot O

key_points: Hardworking commuter school with a grind mentality — not a traditional college experience
Politically active student body; ICE protests, anti-cult posts, and social justice organizing are common
Strong sense of local pride and dark humor holds the community together despite frustrations
key_quotes: I've seen people complain that UNLV has no culture and that they don't feel connected to the campus.
The jaded grad student has officially become the jaded alumnus.
This is why I walk around with earbuds in and/or wear imagery that keeps them from interacting with me.

key_points: **Safety**: December 2023 shooting killed three professors on campus; trauma still raw for students
**Cult Recruitment**: Aggressive cult members and religious solicitors follow, harass, and won't take no for an answer
**Campus Crime**: Vague SA alert emails, reports of homeless individuals on campus, and hit-and-run parking incidents go unresolved
key_quotes: That day was so scary. I remember texting my friends on campus to find out where they were and if they were safe.
One of the Bible study people came up to me... she said it's okay she would follow me to class. Eventually I broke into a run.
Watch out for Hope Church Movement! A notorious cult based out of Arizona that has made its way to UNLV.
If possible don't walk alone, especially women, this is crazy unlv police need to be strict with homeless walking around campus.

key_points: Tradition Keeper Program is a real way to build campus connection most students overlook
Grade distribution data site (myclassgrades.com) lets you pick professors by actual pass rates
UNLV football at Allegiant Stadium is genuinely fun and affordable — best value event in Vegas
key_quotes: The Tradition Keeper Program is a fantastic way to change that. Chasing those passport [stamps]...
I pulled the official grade data for UNLV so you can actually see how hard a professor grades before you pick your classes.
UNLV Football has become one of the best and most affordable things to do in Vegas. Great energy, great atmosphere, and they're actually winning.
